codeReview.summary: The patch fixes the specific builtin `println(...)` case, but it does not fully implement the feature's promised 'truly consuming call boundaries' behavior. Imported stdlib package calls are still treated as consuming in codegen guard suppression, so retained `Drop` values can still silently lose cleanup after package-qualified non-consuming calls.

issues.description: `collect_consumed_idents` still marks every `ExprPath` call as consuming (`ExprCall (ExprPath _, args)`), even though imported stdlib calls also use `ExprPath`. The same file explicitly distinguishes imported package calls elsewhere (`is_imported_package` in `gen_path_call`, lines 1193-1196, and in type inference, lines 375-380), and typecheck routes those calls through `check_stdlib_call` via `check_assoc_fn_call` (`lib/typecheck.ml:709-712`). As a result, a non-consuming call such as `http::listen_and_serve(...)` can still suppress a caller-side Drop guard, so the feature does not yet satisfy 'Drop guards are suppressed only for call arguments whose modeled parameter semantics actually consume ownership.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` as true, but the commit adds a generated runtime artifact `.factory/runtime/non-consuming-call.go` instead of cleaning it up. The skill already says disposable runtime artifacts under `.factory/runtime/` should be cleaned unless the feature explicitly adds tracked fixtures, so this looks like an uncaptured procedure deviation rather than a documentation gap.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/compiler-worker/SKILL.md:29 requires cleanup of disposable runtime artifacts; handoff file `/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/handoffs/2026-04-21T07-59-10-931Z__ownership-non-consuming-call-guard-suppression__a584cb9b-cdad-4a16-91b4-4bfcaf9da3e3.json` reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`; commit `0eab8fa` adds `.factory/runtime/non-consuming-call.go`.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill instructions, shared-state docs, and commit `0eab8fa`. The implementation improves builtin-call behavior but still misclassifies imported package `ExprPath` calls as consuming, so the feature remains incomplete and the review fails.
